name: Dr. Kristin Hook
subject: Happy Labor Day from Dr. Hook!
body:
| | | | | [](https://drkristinhook.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fd888d2bb4a2d4cad917af4b9&id=fa606a543a&e=395a33a3d2)
---
|
# Happy Labor Day!
---
| Today we celebrate the social and economic achievements of American workers,and the labor organizers who have foughtto improve working conditions, wages, and the quality of life for workers!
At the Democratic National Convention, I was so honored to meet one of the fiercest labor leaders of our time –– [Dolores Huerta](https://drkristinhook.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fd888d2bb4a2d4cad917af4b9&id=61ac512e7b&e=395a33a3d2) –– a Chingona who co-founded the United Farm Workers Union with Cesar Chavez to fight for agricultural workersand who masterminded the phrase “**Si, se puede!** ”
---
|
And this past Saturday, I had a wonderful time celebrating Labor Dayat the Kendall County Fair Parade in Boerne, TX with the Kendall County Democrats, Boerne Area Democrats, andDr. Merrie Fox who is running for [Texas Senate District 25](https://drkristinhook.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fd888d2bb4a2d4cad917af4b9&id=582dfdfca1&e=395a33a3d2)!
---
| | | | | | | | | |
---
|
---
| These folks are fired up and ready to FLIP CHIP!I would not be where I am in this campaign today without the help of these amazing people – thank you for all that you do in our district!
**I am committed to ensuring that****every hardworking person****and family in our district can THRIVE!**
To me, this means protecting Social Security and Medicare so that everyone can retire in dignity, raising the minimum wage and addressing wage disparities so everyone is paid fairly,improving access to affordable childcare and paid family and sick leave, and[empowering workers and their right to organize](https://drkristinhook.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fd888d2bb4a2d4cad917af4b9&id=b25de033d8&e=395a33a3d2).
**When I’m elected, I will fight for****EVERY working family here in Texas.**
As a worker, you have our backs, and I hope you know that I’ve got yours too!
In Solidarity Always,
---
[DONATE](https://drkristinhook.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fd888d2bb4a2d4cad917af4b9&id=9b22e16d24&e=395a33a3d2)
|

### The USA horrifyingly ranks worse than Russia and China on economic inequality, and if we hope to prevent catastrophic economic collapse, we need change ASAP
| | [Qasim Rashid](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)

| Sep 2

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/9-facts-on-labor-day-legacy-media?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store)

[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..AOkpeKYLy9x67lpNbtVe2TWFFaHnScX1INrFYWWJPQU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=148407485)
How many people realize that Americans are suffering [worse wealth and income inequality today in 2024 than they did before The Great Depression](https://substack.com/redirect/434206c8-2b58-4b53-a355-6aede862ad7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a century ago? This Labor Day, while politicians and legacy media marvel over how great the Stock Market is doing, I want to provide a clearer lens on the actual state of the economy, what working people are suffering through every day, and how to ensure we better protect economic justice in America.
**Let 's Address This**.
(This Labor Day, I hope you might consider [subscribing to Let's Address This](https://substack.com/redirect/100f9bff-1c2b-4ca1-bbb5-65a5cfe474e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to support my efforts to advance human rights, demand media accountability, and keep you informed on the key issues that impact our lives).
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=148407485&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQ4NDA3NDg1LCJpYXQiOjE3MjUyODkzNzMsImV4cCI6MTcyNzg4MTM3MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.IWF5JOvuQ3ZnF6DHHosdgbcZ7YDsmz1mJofbMSK7lps)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4e9b3e76-9a14-44c3-90f4-5b6db43bf044?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The First Labor Day Parade on September 5, 1982 in New York City. (CREDIT: Department of Labor)
**Fact 1:** Americans are suffering worse inequality today than we did prior to The Great Depression. That inequality in major part enabled an economic collapse, one Americans would not emerge from for over a decade. And if we want to prevent a similar 21st century collapse, we must change course quickly. Notice the below inequality chart, the relatively flat trend from 1940-1980, and then the spike since. This is not by accident. We'll come back to that in a moment.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6200d05b-f0c2-42af-b3a6-7a06c2904450?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Fact 2:** According to, the GINI Index, which measures economic inequality, [the United States ranks last among developed nation on Earth](https://substack.com/redirect/1943b0a7-ca38-4d82-be7a-bf81431d0a25?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In fact, overall we're even ranked worse than Russia or China on economic inequality. What does it say about our economic policies if nations run by autocrats and dictators achieve better economic justice than what our politicians enact?
**Fact 3:** The [so-called "right to work" laws are not just anti-worker, they're pro racist](https://substack.com/redirect/d2aac501-8573-41a3-8f44-f5ff484077a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). "Right to work" laws were designed to uphold white supremacy by protecting employers who refused to hire Black, Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC) workers. These laws were crafted to weaken unions, dismantle collective bargaining, and strip workers of their power. As Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. wisely warned us
> We must guard against being fooled by false slogans, such as 'right to work.' It is a law to rob us of our civil rights and job rights. Its purpose is to destroy labor unions and the freedom of collective bargaining by which unions have improved wages and working conditions of everyone... Wherever these laws have been passed, wages are lower, job opportunities are fewer, and there are no civil rights. We do not intend to let them do this to us. We demand this fraud be stopped. Our weapon is our vote.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..AOkpeKYLy9x67lpNbtVe2TWFFaHnScX1INrFYWWJPQU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=148407485)
**Fact 4:** If the minimum wage had kept pace with inflation and corporate productivity, it would be at least $27 per hour today. Instead, millions of workers are struggling to make ends meet on wages that have stagnated for decades. An analysis by Dean Baker of the Center for Economic Policy and Research placed the minimum wage at $23/hour back in 2021. Given inflation and continued overall productivity growth, it should be at least $27/hour now in 2024.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/150affd6-8e99-43c5-a825-0e3f6cdabad1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Fact 5:** The myth of "trickle-down" economics, propagated by Reaganomics, has been nothing more than a scam that has since 1980 [redistributed an astonishing $50 trillion from the bottom 90% of Americans to the top 1%](https://substack.com/redirect/ab9d44a5-56df-4733-a400-32db76e5055d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Now recall the graph in Fact 1 above, and the spike in inequality since 1980. This shift in economic inequality is not an accident--it's a feature of an economic system designed to favor the wealthy at the expense of everyone else. So how do we fix this?
**Fact 6:** Well, we already have a proven way to fix this. From 1940 to 1980, the wealthiest Americans paid marginal tax rates of 70-94%. None went broke, none left the country, and all remained exceedingly wealthy. During this time, manufacturing boomed, and we witnessed the strongest middle-class growth in U.S. history. It is long past time to return to a system that works for everyone, not just the ultra-rich.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..AOkpeKYLy9x67lpNbtVe2TWFFaHnScX1INrFYWWJPQU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=148407485)
**Fact 7:** And it isn't just low taxes for billion dollar corporations destroying working people, it is also massive wage theft. Experts have long calculated that [corporations continue to steal an estimated $50 billion from workers annually through wage theft](https://substack.com/redirect/6bc1f5d4-5452-4e3e-b9cd-263356ad33a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and other exploitative practices. That's more than all other forms of theft in America--combined. As Economic Policy Institue Vice President Ross Eisenbrey reports:
> Wage theft affects far more people than more well-known crimes such as bank robberies, convenience store robberies, street and highway robberies, and gas station robberies combined, and can be absolutely devastating for workers living from paycheck to paycheck. For low-wage workers, the wages lost from wage theft can total nearly 10 percent of their annual earnings.
**Fact 8:** We need more labor unions because [unions strengthen economic justice](https://substack.com/redirect/8257c465-b3af-4638-ac85-719cbeb2c660?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Unions contribute to 11% or more in increased annual wages, better benefits and paid time off, help close the gender pay gap, and help close the racial pay gap. And ultimately, Unions protect workers against billion dollar corporations. And not just low income workers. Consider that the wealthiest actors and athletes are unionized to ensure that billionaire financiers and team owners cannot exploit them. So why should non-unionized middle-class workers be left vulnerable to the whims of billionaire business owners?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ce560f63-5e01-4de7-806b-0f87242e6add?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Fact 9:** Billionaires don't create jobs, [they exploit workers](https://substack.com/redirect/4093b2a6-9971-431d-8983-9c2a7a5c0ed3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by driving out small businesses, driving down wages, and driving up their own corporate profits. Indeed, [99.9% of businesses are small businesses](https://substack.com/redirect/ab2cb7ec-537e-4804-a318-c8f48e4d0a6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with fewer than 250 employees. Does anyone seriously not see the problem with this [report by OxFam](https://substack.com/redirect/5d5583a0-a933-4505-9aa4-633ad9a9ad74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that billionaires "gained" what workers lost during the pandemic?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7dcd45fc-5489-4692-809c-a583ccf5447d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Let us not forget that America is not $32 trillion in debt because the poor rely too much on welfare and pay too little in taxes. The real reason is that billionaires pay too little in taxes and rely too much on corporate welfare. Billionaire philanthropy, often touted as a noble endeavor, is little more than PR designed to distract from their tax avoidance. The fact is, 90% of today's billionaires were born before Jim Crow laws were abolished, before immigration was open to non-whites, and before women were allowed financial credit. The idea that they "earned it fair and square" is one of the greatest shams of our time. Stop defending billionaires, start taxing them, and start fighting for economic justice in America.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..AOkpeKYLy9x67lpNbtVe2TWFFaHnScX1INrFYWWJPQU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=148407485)
**In Conclusion,** our current economic model is not sustainable. It is unsustainable that 78% of Americans are living paycheck to paycheck. It is unsustainable that 1 in 6 Americans are food insecure. It is unsustainable that 78,000 Americans die annually because we don't guarantee healthcare as a human right. It is unsustainable that while a universal healthcare model would save Americans $450B annually, corporations lobby corrupt politicians to prevent healthcare as a human right. It is unsustainable that Americans have the lowest life expectancy, highest maternal mortality, and highest infant mortality of any developed nation on Earth. And it is unsustainable that as billionaires and the ultra wealthy get wealthier, working people continue to suffer and die sooner. We can fix this, but it requires us to stand united as working people.
This means ensuring we raise the living wage, crack down on union busters, pass the PRO Act, raise corporate taxes, decrease taxes on working people, and demand paid time off and universal healthcare as a human right. This Labor Day, as you enjoy your weekend, your pension, your overtime pay, and the many other benefits that unions have fought for, remember that the fight for economic justice is far from over. We must continue to stand strong, unionize, and demand fair taxation to ensure a future where everyone can thrive.

name: Matt Cartwright
subject: I’m proud to stand with unions
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cartwright for Congress
body:
Today we celebrate all the achievements made by our workforce to create a better, safer working environment.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92021749/495744889/1790176412?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzgyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NWQ5MzA3Yi0zYzY5LWVmMTEtOTkxYS02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2-mtGVZGB6xu0DUwXQhAT1cQJImChegw6vAPLoNkiD8=&emci=efc8e7c6-e266-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&emdi=55d9307b-3c69-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&ceid=23311593) | Happy Labor Day, Peter!

Today we celebrate all the achievements made by our workforce to create a better, safer working environment.

The 40-hour work week, paid sick time, the weekend, and so much more are the result of the hard-won fights from the labor movement. These are wins that we don’t think twice about anymore when we are looking for a new job, or even just entering the workforce for the very first time. **That’s the power of American unions.**

Yet there is still so much more we can do to improve our labor conditions. Parents deserve longer parental leave, and everyone deserves an equitable environment where they are paid what they deserve. Even more importantly, we must strive to ensure the power of unions is protected and expanded at every chance we get.

We owe so much to the labor movement, and I’m proud to fight for our unions every day in Congress. I hope you’ll join me in celebrating the achievements of workers this weekend and always.

-Matt | In 2022, Matt Cartwright was one of only 5 Democrats to win a House race in a district that Donald Trump carried in 2020. He's gearing up for another tough re-election, and he needs your support to keep PA-08 blue. Rush a donation right away to help Matt protect his seat. | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92021750/495744890/-235138218?refcode=em24_240902_labord_fnd_gq_act___mc_ettx&utm_campaign=em24_240902_labord_fnd_gq_act___mc_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=55d9307b-3c69-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzgyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NWQ5MzA3Yi0zYzY5LWVmMTEtOTkxYS02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2-mtGVZGB6xu0DUwXQhAT1cQJImChegw6vAPLoNkiD8=)

name: Katrina Fallick-Wang for Congress
subject: Celebrating Labor Day and Our Journey Together!
body:
Join us in celebrating Labor Day! Discover how you can support our campaign with a phone bank or donation as we champion the values of Utah's workers. | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/92f7fc6dc38c/celebrating-labor-day-and-our-journey-together?e=830e7edf7b)
---
|
---
|
---
| Dear ,
This Labor Day, as we honor the resilience and triumphs of workers and unions across America, I want to take a moment to reflect on the incredible strength and dedication that each of you brings to our campaign. Labor unions have been fundamental in building a stronger, more prosperous America and ensuring that the dignity of work is recognized and rewarded. This is what we fight for in Utah’s 4th District — the prosperity of our families and the recognition of every worker’s contribution to our community.
As we move closer to the election, I am thrilled to share how well our campaign is progressing. Thanks to your tireless efforts and unwavering support, we are on a path that could lead us to a historic victory this November. Every call you make, every door you knock, and every conversation you have brings us one step closer to representing our shared values in Congress.

---
|
### Join Our Phone Bank This Week!
To continue our momentum, I invite you to join our volunteer phone bank this Wednesday or Saturday. Your voice can make a significant difference as we reach out to voters and discuss the crucial issues at stake. Let’s make these sessions our biggest yet!
---
[SIGN UP TO PHONE BANK]()
|
---
|
### Can We Count on Your Support?
If you’re unable to join the phone bank, consider making a donation to our campaign. Every contribution, big or small, powers our grassroots movement and helps us reach more voters every day.
---
[DONATE TODAY](https://katrinaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6fba8715e0ee626118d4f1cfa&id=5537e61716&e=830e7edf7b)
|
---
| This Labor Day, let’s celebrate our hard work and commitment to the values that keep America thriving. Together, we are building a campaign that listens to, respects, and uplifts the voices of Utah families.
Thank you for your continued support and dedication. Let’s keep pushing forward, for our families, for our communities, and for the promise of a better tomorrow.
---
| | | | | | | | | |
---
| | Best wishes, warmest regards,
**Katrina Fallick-Wang** _Candidate for Congress, Utah’s 4th District_

---

name: Colin Van Ostern
subject: We CRUSHED IT!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY COLIN VAN OSTERN FOR CONGRESS

Happy Labor Day! I wasn’t planning on emailing you today, but I wanted to share some good news... **Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, we crushed our August fundraising goal!** I’m so grateful to have your support. Your contribution – no matter the size – means the world to me. We’ve built something special here, and I’m ready to bring it home on the 10th. I hate pairing an ask with a thank you, but with this election this close, _I’ve gotta do it._ [**If you haven’t had a chance to chip in yet, your contribution matters now more than ever. Help us power through the finish line by chipping in $5 or $10 today:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92023818/495748652/-1748132010?amount=10&source=LOC_EM_FR_2024.09.02_EOMTY_B1_F1_S1_C1_30DU_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTIyNTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmRhNzhjMjItM2Y2OS1lZjExLTk5MWEtNjA0NWJkZDllMDk2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=tlMoRzt0x6SamYa9mqfzWKVCaSHJ6FmBaLJ0bTqh9QY=) | | If you’ve saved your payment info with ActBlue, your contribution will be processed immediately.

Peter, our team just got our final August numbers, and we have some bad news to share.

Senator Scott _missed_ his monthly fundraising goal, and we’re hearing reports that his opponent had another big month.

Folks, this isn’t going to cut it. And if we don’t see an increase in support, we’ll lose this race in a shocking defeat.

But don’t worry - we also have some good news.

We were able to secure a **DEADLINE EXTENSION** for the next few hours.

These don’t come lightly but Senate Republicans know this is a race we must win.

**[Here’s how you can help:](http://links.voterickscott.com/u/click?_t=24d1692d4ad14657a18af9ad6ea13243&_m=78fd40bbe45b49128d2ee303c8494a9a&_e=pC1dOIpUGCsQmgLbNFMUhdwVMBb-gfuNKtjyDevFSoW3eae5VClSbV2y7zyAYmK5S1pUcdcdW73VudNjCbVlQwNJuklSJuqGZgXxpVtePRQ1L_iUUvYhx9m6IaKNuroIfWoObqyBt3LPg5rmlxvwwh98YUGBn5pbau2HYWJT9x0BvNxjH6TKxBhRb2_cUUzNk0rDAZxq8BouqvgIu5dgfHZtqpv-My3YOxJtRA_xzWJBSd8K3iVG3mr_7eEAQiC0JHujxWh4ehxHvHJCp6DQRPaGGnrKB_xjGFV7A-rSP4JIWCwcGhn2rIaix7VvXrjAqLo1au9taROC3L1PI3n7eKVdvLoQ4aRURoXTfdeQOxCWWR6221Jyonu3Te2TdI8G)**

body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92024356/495750269/1328194270?source=EM_NBI_BWC_20240902_EN_LaborDay_0_3MO_Bonnie_NA_NA_LaborDay&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NmI5NDZmNS00MDY5LWVmMTEtOTkxYS02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PlS7MmgVJduwL1KWWjb4SeNi7PJpzmJ-F6_tqa4OgSA=&emci=29b71b66-1466-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&emdi=46b946f5-4069-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&ceid=30096098) | Happy Labor Day! However you celebrate – down the Jersey Shore or with loved ones – let us reflect upon the struggles and the achievements of the Labor movement in America.

Thanks to their historic efforts spanning over a century, workers today have more protections, safer working conditions, and fairer wages.

Yet, the work is far from done. I’m working tirelessly to advocate for unions and our workers alongside my colleagues, and I will continue to do so.

As we enjoy this day of rest and relaxation, we owe American laborers a debt of gratitude for what they have done for our country.

Enjoy the holiday.

Bonnie
---
* * *

---
Bonnie Watson Coleman represents New Jersey's 12th district in Congress. She is a strong fighter for progressive values and has dedicated herself to the service of her community.

name: Jon Tester
subject: Can I tell you a story?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
| | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6aab2a77-2c80-4e07-98dd-37459fc4ea88/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Can I tell you a story about my family farm, Peter? [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5cf05fdf-70d3-4d4d-9961-c13fa10f5367/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) In 1910, my grandfather came to Montana. He found a piece of grassy land, took a look at it, and said, “This is the place.” And that’s where the farm began. He farmed the land until the early 1940s, and then passed it down to my folks. In 1978, my wife Sharla and I took over the farm and we’re still here today, three generations and more than 40 years later. We’ve made a lot of progress here — Sharla and I built a new home, several steel buildings, and more storage. The truth is, I love this work. Yes, it makes for early mornings and late nights during planting and harvest seasons — but it also keeps me grounded, and it gives me perspective on the issues we face every day. When I think about climate change, I’m thinking of folks on farms like mine, struggling with catastrophic droughts or historic levels of flooding. When I think about reforming the USPS, I’m thinking of the people in rural communities across Montana who rely on timely mail delivery for their medication or Social Security checks. And when I think about infrastructure, I’m thinking of the rural towns near mine that need access to affordable, high-speed internet. These aren’t abstract issues or talking points. They’re very real challenges that deserve real solutions, and that’s what I’m fighting for in Washington. I spend every single day trying to do right by working families, farmers, students, and veterans here in Montana and across the country. [I’m running for reelection because there’s still work to be done. Just like my parents and grandparents on the family farm, when I commit to something, I see it through. Can you make a split donation between my campaign and Blue Amp Action of any amount today to help me keep up the fight in Washington?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5dd11102-180f-4c05-84e7-a96451296298/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) I’ll leave you with one last truth about farming: Some years you get the right amount of rain, and things work out really well. Other years, you get a whole lot less. That means it’s important to be prepared for any outcome, and to have a support system by your side when things get difficult. We don’t know what this campaign will look like, or what Republicans will throw our way over these final few months. But with your support, we’ll be ready. I appreciate anything you’re able to pitch in. Thanks for your help, and I hope I make you proud. — Jon

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td225c2ee-6789-484e-bbf0-196b6e604d16/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can. | Can I tell you a story about my family farm, Peter? In 1910, my grandfather came to Montana. He found a piece of grassy land, took a look at it, and said, “This is the place.” And that’s where the farm began. He farmed the land until the early 1940s, and then passed it down to my folks. In 1978, my wife Sharla and I took over the farm and we’re still here today, three generations and more than 40 years later. We’ve made a lot of progress here — Sharla and I built a new home, several steel buildings, and more storage. The truth is, I love this work. Yes, it makes for early mornings and late nights during planting and harvest seasons — but it also keeps me grounded, and it gives me perspective on the issues we face every day. When I think about climate change, I’m thinking of folks on farms like mine, struggling with catastrophic droughts or historic levels of flooding. When I think about reforming the USPS, I’m thinking of the people in rural communities across Montana who rely on timely mail delivery for their medication or Social Security checks. And when I think about infrastructure, I’m thinking of the rural towns near mine that need access to affordable, high-speed internet. These aren’t abstract issues or talking points. They’re very real challenges that deserve real solutions, and that’s what I’m fighting for in Washington. I spend every single day trying to do right by working families, farmers, students, and veterans here in Montana and across the country. I’ll leave you with one last truth about farming: Some years you get the right amount of rain, and things work out really well. Other years, you get a whole lot less. That means it’s important to be prepared for any outcome, and to have a support system by your side when things get difficult. We don’t know what this campaign will look like, or what Republicans will throw our way over these final few months. But with your support, we’ll be ready. I appreciate anything you’re able to pitch in. name: Leaders We Deserve
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
This country runs on the day-to-day hard work of union workers in just about every industry.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20240902&t=1&akid=1125%2E587540%2EkKLTG_)
Happy Labor Day, Peter.
This country runs on the day-to-day hard work of union workers in just about every industry. Leaders We Deserve is committed to empowering leaders who legislate for the people and work to strengthen the middle class.
Today, we celebrate the hard work folks put into our country each and every day. Tomorrow, we will continue to bolster the labor movement one candidate at a time.
If you have the day off, please enjoy — and thank you!
Leaders We Deserve

# FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW
---
| | Peter,**I am pleased to share with you that The New York Sun, one of my daily must-read papers, is now the exclusive publisher of my regular columns.

I invite you to consider joining the Sun to receive unlimited access to my latest commentary, alongside some of the finest American journalism.

**But there's also far more to what the Sun is building.

As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.

I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust.

The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored.

The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way.

**The charge to GAP American journalism is being led by the Sun.** A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.**My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly.**

I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths.

They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun coGAP as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts.
---

name: =?UTF-8?Q?Riley_Gaines=2C_Leadership_Institute=C2=A0?=
subject: Help me speak louder
body:
Leftist schools intimidate America’s daughters. Sports leagues like the NCAA let men into women’s sports.
| Friend, I’m Riley Gaines. Maybe you’ve heard me talk about how other female swimmers and I were forced to compete against men -- and even share locker rooms with them.
Most people sat idly by and allowed this injustice to happen. That’s when I realized that nobody else was going to stand up for me, my teammates, and other women like us. I had to speak out. That’s why I launched the **Riley Gaines Center at the Leadership Institute.** My team of Riley Gaines Center Ambassadors and I are building a movement of students, athletes, and concerned citizens who are fed up with the attack on our freedoms and rights. I’d love to have you join us to defy the dangerous gender ideology that’s spreading rampant throughout society today. [Together, you and I will fight against insanity like this: ](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=a7b0c6102d114382accf9b817efd65d1&_e=_ae_5A_1mCA5m7eZcG8W5ufcgsqwoxzMszdyefUo0zHKbpFRlX9rzbMvKtg2XjnU9r5ZPWeqf4ZEHdsZ9TJ1TsPNnvcvPuHkXR3FpU_JpeyyJS5ifjr_4UAL45ilOiRj8JHzIyZn2-sF-1kJGZCBRwGMBgq4EZTovzQvC5-KoJH4J2LSN_Fl8Ol4-5vkia1WHJgGaET1M70c4pm_AL9C2qfMm-i6WhoPkwbLkZBUaC5qQ4lggXoMCrszYcKbVfKRrPeeVKhXfFztIi6DnCzqSroPKdDFpZBv6H0ZcyejfDFReHhWUoU3s89gRLO2lnQUSJ8S5iT4G9Npb8IAfkIVsG_q-A968zS8SibtheVIjVeo8je16E5ZynvXrRqggw7aSJ-kJ0IQR33jKYgzwIeuxbw8ZqpN9_4Q7Adt0U55K0sfrxXOoGkzDbcolDJUuK27AigoiD-FJU15XYL-npm9IlFslLE0FccXiesmdvGaYC9P1EubLauTmHSNejjGesewXXyApmBxawPWPy7SngjK-g%3D%3D) [](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=a7b0c6102d114382accf9b817efd65d1&_e=_ae_5A_1mCA5m7eZcG8W5ufcgsqwoxzMszdyefUo0zHKbpFRlX9rzbMvKtg2XjnU9r5ZPWeqf4ZEHdsZ9TJ1TsPNnvcvPuHkXR3FpU_JpeyyJS5ifjr_4UAL45ilOiRj8JHzIyZn2-sF-1kJGZCBRwGMBgq4EZTovzQvC5-KoJH4J2LSN_Fl8Ol4-5vkia1WHJgGaET1M70c4pm_AL9C2qfMm-i6WhoPkwbLkZBUaC5qQ4lggXoMCrszYcKbVfKRrPeeVKhXfFztIi6DnCzqSroPKdDFpZBv6H0ZcyejfDFReHhWUoU3s89gRLO2lnQUSJ8S5iT4G9Npb8IAfkIVsG_q-A968zS8SibtheVIjVdWV2HOrdJZpQSFsM1VRjDVCIxhDYp_vrujiOcjdNGjW0kOMXaNWGVOs9hjMqzNV8erIBTz0xjK1tFg2xyoTTpwOy-RnlY1vFfJNK_QrGenAxdu9c0OVaQveRutn8L3_PJamBSmtNHZULOrTDhaWogSyDAF5GYJd6W5u7k53BiwhQ%3D%3D) Women already fought for equality. _Now, our achievements are being erased by men who declare themselves women._ Government is making matters worse. Look how the Biden Administration is altering Title IX rules, for instance. Title IX used to protect against discrimination on the basis of sex -- on the incontrovertible, biological fact that men are men and women are women and both are afforded equal access under the law to government-funded programs. But now, the Biden administration’s proposed Title IX changes will stop schools from enforcing women-only recognitions and spaces. They’ll rob women of opportunities, put them in physical danger, and silence them when they dare to dissent. %%first%%, I’ve gotten used to the left trying to shut me up. **But now they’re trying to erase all women**. I’m not backing down from this fight. I hope you won’t either. On behalf of America’s daughters, [ I hope you will stand with me by donating $500, $250, $100, $50, or even $25 right now!](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=a7b0c6102d114382accf9b817efd65d1&_e=_ae_5A_1mCA5m7eZcG8W5ufcgsqwoxzMszdyefUo0zHKbpFRlX9rzbMvKtg2XjnU9r5ZPWeqf4ZEHdsZ9TJ1TsPNnvcvPuHkXR3FpU_JpeyyJS5ifjr_4UAL45ilOiRj8JHzIyZn2-sF-1kJGZCBRwGMBgq4EZTovzQvC5-KoJH4J2LSN_Fl8Ol4-5vkia1WHJgGaET1M70c4pm_AL9C2qfMm-i6WhoPkwbLkZBUaC5qQ4lggXoMCrszYcKbVfKRrPeeVKhXfFztIi6DnCzqSroPKdDFpZBv6H0ZcyejfDFReHhWUoU3s89gRLO2lnQUSJ8S5iT4G9Npb8IAfkIVsG_q-A968zS8SibtheVIjVf3SQqjBDIceC2HZrSEJ93y-Q65yxrPQKBJSO6EYwnSKBHG9-f5sR4PkdcfGnE3i8wITGEMbQwrvdKn4RADP5IauS1qHXQYYIxMWLfEV9sU3Yhj08hZlRHm3Rbhv9mUzLtKP7hZd-x3YnRXVJHjaOY-EyavScdKKQtS1Qds7tfOxA%3D%3D) At this urgent moment, my Riley Gaines Center Ambassadors and I are working to protect the rights of women and girls against the Biden administration’s Title IX changes. We’re taking on the gender ideology radicals who aim to erase women’s place in this country. Patriotic Americans like you who care about women’s rights -- and the truth -- are rising up to stand with us. clean_body: Leftist schools intimidate America’s daughters. Sports leagues like the NCAA let men into women’s sports. | Friend, I’m Riley Gaines. Maybe you’ve heard me talk about how other female swimmers and I were forced to compete against men -- and even share locker rooms with them. Most people sat idly by and allowed this injustice to happen. That’s when I realized that nobody else was going to stand up for me, my teammates, and other women like us. I had to speak out. That’s why I launched the Riley Gaines Center at the Leadership Institute. My team of Riley Gaines Center Ambassadors and I are building a movement of students, athletes, and concerned citizens who are fed up with the attack on our freedoms and rights. I’d love to have you join us to defy the dangerous gender ideology that’s spreading rampant throughout society today. Women already fought for equality. Now, our achievements are being erased by men who declare themselves women. Government is making matters worse. Look how the Biden Administration is altering Title IX rules, for instance. Title IX used to protect against discrimination on the basis of sex -- on the incontrovertible, biological fact that men are men and women are women and both are afforded equal access under the law to government-funded programs. But now, the Biden administration’s proposed Title IX changes will stop schools from enforcing women-only recognitions and spaces. They’ll rob women of opportunities, put them in physical danger, and silence them when they dare to dissent. %%first%%, I’ve gotten used to the left trying to shut me up. But now they’re trying to erase all women. I’m not backing down from this fight. I hope you won’t either. On behalf of America’s daughters, At this urgent moment, my Riley Gaines Center Ambassadors and I are working to protect the rights of women and girls against the Biden administration’s Title IX changes. We’re taking on the gender ideology radicals who aim to erase women’s place in this country. Patriotic Americans like you who care about women’s rights -- and the truth -- are rising up to stand with us. Together, you and I can stop the left’s attacks, and save women’s place in America’s future. Sincerely, Riley Gaines Director, The Riley Gaines Center at the Leadership Institute | The Leadership Institute, 1101 N. Highland St., Steven P.J. Wood Building, Arlington, VA 22201, United States, (703) 247-2000

I’m sorry I had to send you this e-mail on Labor Day. We just received the fundraising numbers for August, and we couldn’t wait. | Peter,

I’m sorry I had to send you this e-mail on Labor Day. We just received the fundraising numbers for August, and we couldn’t wait.

**We fell just short of our monthly fundraising objective by just $504.**

I know that doesn’t sound like a lot for a Senate campaign, but it could be the difference between victory and defeat in a race that will come down to just a few thousand votes.

We have less than 24 hours to erase our August fundraising shortfall before the end of Labor Day at MIDNIGHT TONIGHT. [**Will you send a quick contribution of $15, $25, $100 or more today?**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=164d9d32f72b4850a357662ac694e692&_e=R8sup6_zQo02049oiXcx6cDgtENO4nPX7eblIG1BaIbCxFiwW4U5pbM9zt7s2sn_xCyvrjIYB8LbcGzzFuNobauQVTkqpL6TH2elEekBNP_uR2jmPsOUen1Ibda51TJs31f2QHwn-ux3CzEdkNkr-3Cy5UOmAms3jC14P36fuMQuRM_7o-_hM4NkLQW7HziP5fsga-d9tSS5mqOJBGOW9jal2sHP370-EC2LigyrD_Zn1tMvKHqNLDa5DPtR3ETspVomw4jwWEQKt_o7HTzVZPSUs0V4TqPQLcP1jVry-aVn8DGIx5XquwXun1ghXgHcgvkazmD3dUmVXjz_mDosTmoCgmLfHS664a40tv6LPfReI0ABL1bw21c21QeM6IoLUPg4OmCc8yCdg9Dh7ewKjzQOFL3baPN2aexNj-4pa5Y%3D)

| **MEET ERIC**

Eric's a fourth-generation Wisconsinite, born and raised in the Madison area. He's a serial entrepreneur. He founded and managed his first business in his twenties and then went on to start and build many others.

When he is not running his businesses, he is focused on his family and his philanthropic endeavors. He and his brother Steve operate 10 Hovde Homes around the world that provide shelter, supportive services and love to vulnerable children.

clean_body: I’m sorry I had to send you this e-mail on Labor Day. We just received the fundraising numbers for August, and we couldn’t wait. | Peter, I’m sorry I had to send you this e-mail on Labor Day. We just received the fundraising numbers for August, and we couldn’t wait. We fell just short of our monthly fundraising objective by just $504. I know that doesn’t sound like a lot for a Senate campaign, but it could be the difference between victory and defeat in a race that will come down to just a few thousand votes. We have less than 24 hours to erase our August fundraising shortfall before the end of Labor Day at MIDNIGHT TONIGHT. Thank you, Eric Hovde Republican for U.S. Senate P.S. | MEET ERIC Eric's a fourth-generation Wisconsinite, born and raised in the Madison area. He's a serial entrepreneur. He founded and managed his first business in his twenties and then went on to start and build many others. When he is not running his businesses, he is focused on his family and his philanthropic endeavors. He and his brother Steve operate 10 Hovde Homes around the world that provide shelter, supportive services and love to vulnerable children. Join the Conversation: | Paid for by Hovde for Wisconsin

The full September Field Series includes: September 2 - Get Out The Vote for Jill & Butch

name: Heidi Campbell
subject: Honoring Labor Day in Tennessee
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Campbell for State Senate, Lee Jones, treasurer
body:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/ANuxmAsWB0CHLK9S_7eA4Q2?contactdata=P9DSYI48+lmgBO1OPeHshneG6vvIb4X5kWa0YBFisE187j/KJBmwA9/dDma+d/gEpKJvGImOnF48PgNsv8oHFRnYK3t4qwBy17nwsf1w5NdGMVqM7IxnpMG56oRB+d2x&quick=true [https://secure.ngpvan.com/ANuxmAsWB0CHLK9S_7eA4Q2?contactdata=P9DSYI48+lmgBO1OPeHshneG6vvIb4X5kWa0YBFisE187j/KJBmwA9/dDma+d/gEpKJvGImOnF48PgNsv8oHFRnYK3t4qwBy17nwsf1w5NdGMVqM7IxnpMG56oRB+d2x&quick=true]
Happy Labor Day, Peter!
I’m proud to be a voice fighting for workers rights at the TN State Legislature. Every Tennessean deserves fair pay, safe working conditions, and quality benefits. When our workers are treated with dignity and respect, our entire state thrives.
We must protect pro-labor, pro-worker voices in our legislature. That is why I’m asking for you to consider lending your support today to ensure my re-election.
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/re-elect-heidi-email]
I hope you take this opportunity to rest, reflect, and honor the American Labor Movement continuously fighting to improve working conditions in our state & country. I will always stand with Tennesseans organizing and advocating for the freedom and dignity of all workers.
Sen. Heidi Campbell
Senator Heidi Campbell is running for re-election in TN Senate District 20. She is championing change for Tennessee and won't rest until we see gun safety reform, corporate accountability, and a safe and prosperous community where everyone can thrive.

clean_body: [] Happy Labor Day, Peter! I’m proud to be a voice fighting for workers rights at the TN State Legislature. Every Tennessean deserves fair pay, safe working conditions, and quality benefits. When our workers are treated with dignity and respect, our entire state thrives. We must protect pro-labor, pro-worker voices in our legislature. That is why I’m asking for you to consider lending your support today to ensure my re-election. DONATE [] I hope you take this opportunity to rest, reflect, and honor the American Labor Movement continuously fighting to improve working conditions in our state & country. I will always stand with Tennesseans organizing and advocating for the freedom and dignity of all workers. Sen. Heidi Campbell Senator Heidi Campbell is running for re-election in TN Senate District 20. She is championing change for Tennessee and won't rest until we see gun safety reform, corporate accountability, and a safe and prosperous community where everyone can thrive. name: Yadira Caraveo
subject: Why I fight for them
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Caraveo for Congress
body:
I'm proud to follow in their footsteps

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92025661/495753701/1561815853?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMTRlYWNjNS00NDY5LWVmMTEtOTkxYS02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JVersJrMoKG8Ys2kpnqmzaVcDa6A0ob6dIiGU_MXDiw=&emci=67631c0e-fe66-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&emdi=b14eacc5-4469-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&ceid=12666102) Hi Peter, Labor Day has always held a special place in my heart. When my parents came to Colorado from a small town in Mexico, they had dreams of a better life for their family. Through hard work, they raised me and my three siblings in Adams County, all on a construction worker’s pay. Thanks to their sacrifice, all four of us were able to go to college and graduate. Their sacrifices are a reminder of the American Dream – a dream that every hardworking person in this country deserves the chance to achieve. In medical school, I found my voice as a leader, becoming a union representative with SEIU. That’s why I’m dedicated to fighting for workers every day. I’m proud to stand with the unions and activists who have fought to protect workers for generations. It’s why I helped pass a bipartisan tax cut, expanded paid sick and family leave, fought Big Pharma to decrease prescription prices, and increased funding for early childhood education. [_**But I need your support to keep pushing for these crucial changes. Can you chip in to help us keep fighting for hardworking Americans?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92025662/495753702/-1837108903?refcode=email_090224_Labor-Day&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMTRlYWNjNS00NDY5LWVmMTEtOTkxYS02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JVersJrMoKG8Ys2kpnqmzaVcDa6A0ob6dIiGU_MXDiw=) | | [Donate Now →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92025663/495753703/-1837108903?refcode=email_090224_Labor-Day&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMTRlYWNjNS00NDY5LWVmMTEtOTkxYS02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JVersJrMoKG8Ys2kpnqmzaVcDa6A0ob6dIiGU_MXDiw=)
---
Have a happy and safe Labor Day!
Thanks,
Yadira

When you love this country, you fight for the people who make it work.
| | |

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92025884/495754295/-1893289703?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk2685-fr-nd&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzQ3YTIyNi00NTY5LWVmMTEtOTkxYS02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fpUR5B_lX59F0_w2Fw4oxSkZoQdlk0HUsaUTfg4u2HY=)
Happy Labor Day, friend!
Whether you punch a clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or work for tips, care for children or an aging parent – thanks for all you do.
Today, we celebrate the people who make this country work, and we honor the workers who, for generations, fought and sacrificed to secure Americans’ right to organize for fair pay, safe working conditions, good benefits, and a voice in their workplace. **All work has dignity, and I’ll never stop fighting to make sure that hard work pays off for everyone.**
But my MAGA opponent thinks differently. From eliminating overtime protections to ending unions as we know them, he’s backing every he’s backing terrible pieces of Trump and Vance’s Project 2025. It’s an affront to the good folks of Ohio and across America who work hard every day to support themselves and their families.
**I’m running for reelection to continue fighting for the Dignity of Work – all work. But the truth is, I’m in the fight of my life to defend Ohio’s seat and our Democratic Senate majority._CNN_ is calling me one of the most vulnerable senators on the ballot, and my MAGA opponent is outspending me on TV ads.**
**That’s why I’m personally asking for your help, friend. [Will you chip in $5 or more before midnight so we can get our pro-worker message out across Ohio, win this race, and keep the Senate blue?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92025885/495754296/-1893289703?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk2685-fr-nd&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzQ3YTIyNi00NTY5LWVmMTEtOTkxYS02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fpUR5B_lX59F0_w2Fw4oxSkZoQdlk0HUsaUTfg4u2HY=)**

name: Jahana Hayes
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/92026044/495754684/-1295403277?refcode=AU_JH_CL_GEN_TYE_AC90_20240902_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZjAwMmRlMi00NTY5LWVmMTEtOTkxYS02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0Dftru4-pegVLGTfj16yRdsYiYOwR_XKqlyFzxuAi-8=&emci=ee215313-4269-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&emdi=df002de2-4569-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&ceid=15529135) | Happy Labor Day, friend! I have been crisscrossing CT-05 meeting with working people and hearing directly about the policies you want prioritized in Congress to build our economy and strengthen the middle class. As a labor champion, my commitment to labor did not begin or end with on Labor Day. **It began long before that.** **As a teacher and union member running against George Logan, a lobbyist and long-time director at Aquarion, a billion-dollar water utility, there is no question which candidate stands on the side of labor.**
* In Congress, **I strongly support the _Raise the Wage Act_ **to increase the federal minimum wage from $7.25 per hour. In the Connecticut Senate, **George Logan voted against raising the minimum wage in CT**.
* In Congress, **I strongly support paid family and medical leave**. In the Connecticut Senate, **George Logan voted against it.**
* In Congress, **I strongly support the _PRO Act_ **and the right of workers to form a union and collectively bargain for their wages and benefits. In the Connecticut Senate, **George Logan voted against a state police contract that would have raised wages and provided hazard pay for the law enforcement officers who keep us safe**.

If that is not enough, Logan has dismissed raising the minimum wage, passing paid family and medical leave, and strengthening labor rights in Congress as unnecessary **because Connecticut already has them**. The thing is, _he opposed those policies**despite** their passage_. It just goes to show how George Logan has always been out-of-step with the people of CT-05, and he does not share the priorities of the labor movement and working people in Congress. Labor unions have given us weekends, child labor laws, the 8-hour workday, the 5-day work week, and so much more. As your voice in Congress, I will continue to advocate for organized labor, unions, and the rights and protections we have worked so hard for to build and strengthen the middle class. Thanks for reading, and happy Labor Day. With immense gratitude, Jahana Hayes

clean_body: | | Happy Labor Day, friend! I have been crisscrossing CT-05 meeting with working people and hearing directly about the policies you want prioritized in Congress to build our economy and strengthen the middle class. As a labor champion, my commitment to labor did not begin or end with on Labor Day. It began long before that. As a teacher and union member running against George Logan, a lobbyist and long-time director at Aquarion, a billion-dollar water utility, there is no question which candidate stands on the side of labor. In Congress, I strongly support the Raise the Wage Act to increase the federal minimum wage from $7.25 per hour. In the Connecticut Senate, George Logan voted against raising the minimum wage in CT. In Congress, I strongly support paid family and medical leave. In the Connecticut Senate, George Logan voted against it. In Congress, I strongly support the PRO Act and the right of workers to form a union and collectively bargain for their wages and benefits. In the Connecticut Senate, George Logan voted against a state police contract that would have raised wages and provided hazard pay for the law enforcement officers who keep us safe. If that is not enough, Logan has dismissed raising the minimum wage, passing paid family and medical leave, and strengthening labor rights in Congress as unnecessary because Connecticut already has them. The thing is, he opposed those policiesdespite their passage. It just goes to show how George Logan has always been out-of-step with the people of CT-05, and he does not share the priorities of the labor movement and working people in Congress. Labor unions have given us weekends, child labor laws, the 8-hour workday, the 5-day work week, and so much more. As your voice in Congress, I will continue to advocate for organized labor, unions, and the rights and protections we have worked so hard for to build and strengthen the middle class. Thanks for reading, and happy Labor Day. With immense gratitude, Jahana Hayes | | About Jahana Jahana Hayes proudly represents Connecticut's 5th Congressional district. name: Nancy Pelosi
subject: Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nancy Pelosi for Congress
body:
---
| | | |
---

---

Dear Friends,
On Labor Day and every day, Democrats lift up America’s union workers as the heart of our economy and the foundation of opportunity, strength and greatness in our country.
It’s better in a union. Democrats know and more and more Americans are learning every day that a strong middle class is the backbone of our Democracy and that our middle class has a union label on it.
As unions continue to build power for workers at the picket line and at the bargaining table, Republicans are preparing to implement an anti-worker and anti-Democracy manifesto – Project 2025.
Project 2025 would gut workers’ rights, ban public employee unions, abolish civil service protections, and allow states to ban labor unions, end overtime pay requirements and ignore child labor laws and the federal minimum wage.
The late former President of the United Auto Workers, Walter Reuther, saw this threat clearly decades ago and warned that: “There's a direct relationship between the ballot box and the bread box, and what the union fights for and wins at the bargaining table can be taken away in the legislative halls.”
With the help of our Labor partners reaching out to voters and owning the ground from now until November 5th, Democrats will take back the House electing Hakeem Jeffries as Speaker, win a working majority in the Senate and elect Kamala Harris as President and Tim Walz as Vice President of the United States.
We know the stakes are high and the campaign will be difficult — but we don’t agonize, we organize and WE UNIONIZE!
Happy Labor Day, from my family to yours! God bless you and God bless America!
NANCY

Speaker Emerita Nancy Pelosi is the U.S. Representative for California’s 11th District. She's fighting to help working families and protect America from the dangerous agenda of House Republicans. We know you're getting a lot of emails -- but it's only because what we're facing right now as a country is so important. # [I started a rumor on TikTok and Twitter. ](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=283449&post_id=148414076&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=false&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQ4NDE0MDc2LCJpYXQiOjE3MjUyOTQ0MzgsImV4cCI6MTcyNzg4NjQzOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4MzQ0OSIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.mitfjVweAW3uGxCwRs951cnK1hEkeOJD1hDVnM_i6ok)
### In less than 90 minutes, news sites were reporting it as fact.
| Sep 2
---|---
---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=283449&post_id=148414076&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=false&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQ4NDE0MDc2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTcyNTI5NDQzOCwiZXhwIjoxNzI3ODg2NDM4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjgzNDQ5Iiwic3ViIjoicmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.frIxBUmZSfvtQCAgL9xs3JigIEkt0y05tgMw3bFHL6Q&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=283449&post_id=148414076&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=false&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQ4NDE0MDc2LCJpYXQiOjE3MjUyOTQ0MzgsImV4cCI6MTcyNzg4NjQzOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4MzQ0OSIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.mitfjVweAW3uGxCwRs951cnK1hEkeOJD1hDVnM_i6ok&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=283449&post_id=148414076&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=false&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQ4NDE0MDc2LCJpYXQiOjE3MjUyOTQ0MzgsImV4cCI6MTcyNzg4NjQzOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4MzQ0OSIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.mitfjVweAW3uGxCwRs951cnK1hEkeOJD1hDVnM_i6ok)

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/rebekahjones/p/i-started-a-rumor-on-tiktok-and-twitter?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store)
---
Donald Trump's staff [assaulted an employee](https://substack.com/redirect/cfa3f580-7854-48f5-acf1-09a96a5951d6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at Arlington National Cemetery and broke multiple laws while using the site for campaigning. They [desecrated graves](https://substack.com/redirect/b6e412dd-6607-4753-ab84-697d8ffd44e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) when their fleet of SUV's drove and parked over them. Donald Trump[ posed](https://substack.com/redirect/d8f7b9da-f2f4-4891-8bca-349fd5a78601?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) next to the family of the deceased smiling and giving a thumbs-up.
That wasn't the plan, though. That wasn't what they wanted the story to be about.
The plan was to [falsely claim](https://substack.com/redirect/1a838b94-9164-468a-a2a6-71a5e0799f9d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that Vice President Kamala Harris skipped a public event at Arlington as a sign of disrespect to the fallen soldiers.
There was no public event and campaigning (and even most photography) is banned at Arlington.
If not for the disturbing events taking place that day, Trump's team would have successfully spread a lie meant to harm Harris (though they still tried anyway, with little success).
The far-right creates false stories and uses propaganda sites to back it up all of the time - there are entire Twitter accounts dedicated just to doing that _to me_ (yes, four years later).
The organized disinformation machine is somewhat synchronous - coordinated efforts to promote a lie they know is a lie by intentionally manipulating the algorithms.
But that's not the only kind of rumor that spreads.
Then there are accidental rumors, too, like the joke about JD Vance's sofa-sexual relations, started in jest by a relatively unknown Twitter user.
The Vance joke was obviously always that - a joke.
What if a less-obviously false rumor targeting the GOP took off? Could it manipulate the algorithm and media to propagate the lie? What would the data say about the speed and nature of a rumor if you had full access to the account data of the person starting the rumor?
The difficulty in measuring how a rumor or disinformation spreads online is that we're always outside observers. Few with a platform as large as mine are willing to engage in social experiments of this nature because of the obvious ethical questions and because they don't want to lose followers.
As someone unafraid to lose followers and well-versed in the mechanisms social media uses to spread disinformation, I started a rumor and carefully monitored its reach.
### **The rumor: Melania Trump had filed for divorce.**
I picked something rather innocuous - Trump's already been divorced multiple times and he was convicted of 34 felony counts earlier this year for trying to hide his affair with Stormy Daniels. It wouldn't _surprise_ anyone, and I doubt it would have much of an impact overall in the election if she did file for divorce.
I provided almost no details. I even named the wrong Florida county initially (they live in Palm Beach, I said Miami-Dade). I made up a random time (5:12 pm) and said the filing was made through the e-file court system, which allows anyone to file any civil action 24 hours, 7 days a week.
Because the court systems take days to reflect these filings, there was no easy way to prove the story was false absent a public statement by Melania herself. I posted something that would require a direct statement from the subject in order to disprove. _It could not be fact-checked._
### **Community Notes **
Community Notes, a user-driven way to add context (or disinformation) to anyone's tweets, tried and failed to fact-check the claim, anyway.
I've discussed how unreliable Twitter's "Community Notes" program can be in the past. Since anyone can sign up to add and vote on Community Notes, the system is prone to the same kinds of disinformation and lies that the rest of the site struggles with.
If a Republican posts a lie and someone suggests a community note be added, the Republicans within Community Notes will down-vote it so that it isn't posted. If a Democrat posts something true, Republicans will furiously add notes to the post, which limits the reach of the tweet even if no notes are actually added or approved.
The community note added to the post about Trump's divorce filing stated that a search for filings for Melania Trump yielded no results and that courts were closed on Sunday, and then referenced a known disinformation and stalker account's post on Twitter as a "verified and trustworthy" source.
Neither of those things disproves the rumor. A filing not being shown in a system that takes several days to process is absence of proof, not proof of absence. The e-filing system is active 24/7, so courts being closed is irrelevant. The Twitter account linked to in the note is one of many serial stalkers I have on the site, and regularly posts pro-GOP propaganda.
The Community Note was as false as the post itself.
### **The Media**
"Back in the old days" the media would fact-check such claims before sharing information on a story of national importance. Not today. Not on Twitter.
Multiple journalists shared the tweet, with only a few adding "unconfirmed for now but we're looking into it."
Within an hour, journalists from ABC, NBC, USA Today, and several other major media outlets had retweeted the rumor with no commentary or fact checking.
The only aspect that could be proven to be false was a manipulated Newsweek screenshot repeating the rumor I posted underneath the original tweet nearly 20 minutes later - one I so crassly created that the word "divorce" was underlined because of a formatting error.
BBC reporter Shayan Sardarizadeh correctly identified where the image originated from and posted a fact check to his followers at 7:02 PM - 27 minutes after the altered image was added to the thread.
But not one journalist reached out to me for proof before reposting the rumor. Not one.
Two influential Twitter users did, though - Wajahat Ali of [THE LEFT HOOK with Wajahat Ali](https://open.substack.com/users/5345375-the-left-hook-with-wajahat-ali?utm_source=mentions) and Brian Krassenstein. They were among a cohort of people I gave the heads up to, explaining the experiment and asking them to refrain from commenting.
A handful of users who specialize in online disinformation asked for evidence in the comments below the post with the rumor, but with hundreds of comments piling up, I didn't see most of them until after the experiment was over. I informed those whose requests I saw of what was going on.
The creators and influencers of Twitter did more to verify the claim before sharing the rumor than any media personality or reporter.
That's not even the worst of it.
What shocked me most was that, in less than 90 minutes, a seemingly legitimate-looking news site had posted the [rumor as fact and had an entire article to go along with it](https://substack.com/redirect/18dbb8af-7fba-491e-909c-3ca636929218?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). I now had "proof" by way of an AI-generated news aggregation website that my rumor was true, even though my rumor was the entire basis of the article.
I had a link I could add to substantiate the claim and I didn't have to provide even a shred of evidence or even talk to a journalist to get it. That accelerated the spread and reach of the rumor like a shot of adrenaline.
### **The data**
The initial take-off was slower than I expected, but the overall speed and reach of the rumor surpassed my expectations, amplified by retweets from users with more than 50,000 followers, and completely unhindered by Community Notes.
The first post about the rumor went live at 5:58 PM. I initially listed the wrong county.
At 6:09, the first post had 4,200 "impressions." I posted a second tweet changing the county and specifying that the divorce was e-filed with a time stamp within the hour.
At 6:25 PM, the post had about 11,000 "impressions." I added the altered Newsweek image.
At 6:26 PM, a reporter retweeted the post to his 61,000 followers.
At 6:33, the post had 73,356 "impressions" with more than 1,000 likes and 415 retweets.
At 6:57, almost exactly one hour after the initial post, the tweet had 156,000 "impressions," 2,000 likes and 702 retweets.
At 7:02 pm, Sardarizadeh from the BBC posted his fast-check.
At 7:14 pm, a proposed community note was still pending on the Newsweek image and the original rumor.
At 7:21 pm, the first AI-generated news site confirms the rumor.
At 7:34 PM, the original (wrong county) post reaches 202,000 impressions, while the updated post hits 426,000 impressions.
At 7:58 PM, a second AI-driven website posts that they've confirmed the story through "local news reports." Within the next six minutes, another 240,000 impressions.
By 8:26 pm, the various versions of the rumor garnered more than two millions impressions from my tweets alone - that does not include visibility from accounts that posted the information without linking directly to my tweet.
I intended to leave the rumor up overnight, but after a few hours and millions of views later, I exposed the ruse at 8:58 PM. The entire experiment lasted exactly three hours.
I'm planning on creating a visual timeline of how this happens so quickly, which accounts most contributed to its amplification, and which mechanisms most hindered its spread (a community note with false information that links to a propaganda account has no impact, apparently).
There's a lot of data to work with here, and my hope is that we can use it to counteract and hopefully kill false information more judiciously in the future.
I accepted going into this project that I'd likely lose followers and some of my fans' trust. Seeing how personal information becomes weaponized propaganda has impacted some of my friends online recently, I decided to go forward with the project.
I gained followers, returned my account to its private status, and shared the details on TikTok. Speaking of which....
### **TikTok**
I posted a screenshot of the divorce tweet with all relevant hashtags to TikTok exactly one hour after I posted it on Twitter to see how a different platform handled this kind of information.
Unlike Twitter, TikTok demoted the post almost immediately and it only reached about 3,500 people.

clean_body: In less than 90 minutes, news sites were reporting it as fact. | | ---|---|--- | | | ? ? In less than 90 minutes, news sites were reporting it as fact. | Sep 2 ---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | Donald Trump's staff at Arlington National Cemetery and broke multiple laws while using the site for campaigning. They when their fleet of SUV's drove and parked over them. Donald Trump next to the family of the deceased smiling and giving a thumbs-up. That wasn't the plan, though. That wasn't what they wanted the story to be about. The plan was to that Vice President Kamala Harris skipped a public event at Arlington as a sign of disrespect to the fallen soldiers. There was no public event and campaigning (and even most photography) is banned at Arlington. If not for the disturbing events taking place that day, Trump's team would have successfully spread a lie meant to harm Harris (though they still tried anyway, with little success). The far-right creates false stories and uses propaganda sites to back it up all of the time - there are entire Twitter accounts dedicated just to doing that to me (yes, four years later). The organized disinformation machine is somewhat synchronous - coordinated efforts to promote a lie they know is a lie by intentionally manipulating the algorithms. But that's not the only kind of rumor that spreads. Then there are accidental rumors, too, like the joke about JD Vance's sofa-sexual relations, started in jest by a relatively unknown Twitter user. The Vance joke was obviously always that - a joke. What if a less-obviously false rumor targeting the GOP took off? Could it manipulate the algorithm and media to propagate the lie? What would the data say about the speed and nature of a rumor if you had full access to the account data of the person starting the rumor? The difficulty in measuring how a rumor or disinformation spreads online is that we're always outside observers. Few with a platform as large as mine are willing to engage in social experiments of this nature because of the obvious ethical questions and because they don't want to lose followers. As someone unafraid to lose followers and well-versed in the mechanisms social media uses to spread disinformation, I started a rumor and carefully monitored its reach. The rumor: Melania Trump had filed for divorce. I picked something rather innocuous - Trump's already been divorced multiple times and he was convicted of 34 felony counts earlier this year for trying to hide his affair with Stormy Daniels. It wouldn't surprise anyone, and I doubt it would have much of an impact overall in the election if she did file for divorce. I provided almost no details. I even named the wrong Florida county initially (they live in Palm Beach, I said Miami-Dade). I made up a random time (5:12 pm) and said the filing was made through the e-file court system, which allows anyone to file any civil action 24 hours, 7 days a week. Because the court systems take days to reflect these filings, there was no easy way to prove the story was false absent a public statement by Melania herself. I posted something that would require a direct statement from the subject in order to disprove. It could not be fact-checked. Community Notes Community Notes, a user-driven way to add context (or disinformation) to anyone's tweets, tried and failed to fact-check the claim, anyway. I've discussed how unreliable Twitter's "Community Notes" program can be in the past. Since anyone can sign up to add and vote on Community Notes, the system is prone to the same kinds of disinformation and lies that the rest of the site struggles with. If a Republican posts a lie and someone suggests a community note be added, the Republicans within Community Notes will down-vote it so that it isn't posted. If a Democrat posts something true, Republicans will furiously add notes to the post, which limits the reach of the tweet even if no notes are actually added or approved. The community note added to the post about Trump's divorce filing stated that a search for filings for Melania Trump yielded no results and that courts were closed on Sunday, and then referenced a known disinformation and stalker account's post on Twitter as a "verified and trustworthy" source. Neither of those things disproves the rumor. A filing not being shown in a system that takes several days to process is absence of proof, not proof of absence. The e-filing system is active 24/7, so courts being closed is irrelevant. The Twitter account linked to in the note is one of many serial stalkers I have on the site, and regularly posts pro-GOP propaganda. The Community Note was as false as the post itself. The Media "Back in the old days" the media would fact-check such claims before sharing information on a story of national importance. Not today. Not on Twitter. Multiple journalists shared the tweet, with only a few adding "unconfirmed for now but we're looking into it." Within an hour, journalists from ABC, NBC, USA Today, and several other major media outlets had retweeted the rumor with no commentary or fact checking. The only aspect that could be proven to be false was a manipulated Newsweek screenshot repeating the rumor I posted underneath the original tweet nearly 20 minutes later - one I so crassly created that the word "divorce" was underlined because of a formatting error. BBC reporter Shayan Sardarizadeh correctly identified where the image originated from and posted a fact check to his followers at 7:02 PM - 27 minutes after the altered image was added to the thread. But not one journalist reached out to me for proof before reposting the rumor. Not one. Two influential Twitter users did, though - Wajahat Ali of and Brian Krassenstein. They were among a cohort of people I gave the heads up to, explaining the experiment and asking them to refrain from commenting. A handful of users who specialize in online disinformation asked for evidence in the comments below the post with the rumor, but with hundreds of comments piling up, I didn't see most of them until after the experiment was over. I informed those whose requests I saw of what was going on. The creators and influencers of Twitter did more to verify the claim before sharing the rumor than any media personality or reporter. That's not even the worst of it. What shocked me most was that, in less than 90 minutes, a seemingly legitimate-looking news site had posted the . I now had "proof" by way of an AI-generated news aggregation website that my rumor was true, even though my rumor was the entire basis of the article. I had a link I could add to substantiate the claim and I didn't have to provide even a shred of evidence or even talk to a journalist to get it. That accelerated the spread and reach of the rumor like a shot of adrenaline. The data The initial take-off was slower than I expected, but the overall speed and reach of the rumor surpassed my expectations, amplified by retweets from users with more than 50,000 followers, and completely unhindered by Community Notes. The first post about the rumor went live at 5:58 PM. I initially listed the wrong county. At 6:09, the first post had 4,200 "impressions." I posted a second tweet changing the county and specifying that the divorce was e-filed with a time stamp within the hour. At 6:25 PM, the post had about 11,000 "impressions." I added the altered Newsweek image. At 6:26 PM, a reporter retweeted the post to his 61,000 followers. At 6:33, the post had 73,356 "impressions" with more than 1,000 likes and 415 retweets. At 6:57, almost exactly one hour after the initial post, the tweet had 156,000 "impressions," 2,000 likes and 702 retweets. At 7:02 pm, Sardarizadeh from the BBC posted his fast-check. At 7:14 pm, a proposed community note was still pending on the Newsweek image and the original rumor. At 7:21 pm, the first AI-generated news site confirms the rumor. At 7:34 PM, the original (wrong county) post reaches 202,000 impressions, while the updated post hits 426,000 impressions. At 7:58 PM, a second AI-driven website posts that they've confirmed the story through "local news reports." Within the next six minutes, another 240,000 impressions. By 8:26 pm, the various versions of the rumor garnered more than two millions impressions from my tweets alone - that does not include visibility from accounts that posted the information without linking directly to my tweet. I intended to leave the rumor up overnight, but after a few hours and millions of views later, I exposed the ruse at 8:58 PM. The entire experiment lasted exactly three hours. I'm planning on creating a visual timeline of how this happens so quickly, which accounts most contributed to its amplification, and which mechanisms most hindered its spread (a community note with false information that links to a propaganda account has no impact, apparently). There's a lot of data to work with here, and my hope is that we can use it to counteract and hopefully kill false information more judiciously in the future. I accepted going into this project that I'd likely lose followers and some of my fans' trust. Seeing how personal information becomes weaponized propaganda has impacted some of my friends online recently, I decided to go forward with the project. I gained followers, returned my account to its private status, and shared the details on TikTok. Speaking of which.... TikTok I posted a screenshot of the divorce tweet with all relevant hashtags to TikTok exactly one hour after I posted it on Twitter to see how a different platform handled this kind of information. Unlike Twitter, TikTok demoted the post almost immediately and it only reached about 3,500 people.
